Sewer Camera Inspection in Chicago, IL

Licensed Plumbing Professionals • Available 24/7

Call 833-758-6911
Home › Services › Sewer Camera Inspection
Licensed & Insured 24/7 Service Serving Chicagoland

Sewer Camera Inspection

A sewer camera inspection lets us see exactly what's happening inside your sewer line without digging. We push a waterproof camera through the pipe on a flexible cable and watch the live feed. It shows roots, cracks, offsets, grease buildup, bellies, and blockages — and tells us exactly where the problem is.

When to Get a Camera Inspection

  • Recurring drain or sewer backups — to find the root cause
  • Buying a home — especially in Chicago where sewer lines are often 50+ years old
  • After sewer cleaning — to confirm the line is clear and check condition
  • Before a remodel — to assess the line before adding fixtures
  • Sewage odor that can't be explained by a simple clog

What the Camera Reveals

  • Root intrusion: Roots growing through pipe joints — very common in clay lines
  • Cracks and fractures: From settling, frost heave, or age
  • Bellies (sags): Low spots where water and debris collect
  • Offsets: Pipe sections shifted apart at joints
  • Pipe material and condition: Clay, cast iron, Orangeburg, PVC — we identify what you have

Typical Camera Inspection Costs

  • Standard inspection: $150–$400
  • With sewer cleaning (bundled): $350–$700 combined
  • Locator service (marking pipe path): $100–$200 add-on

A camera inspection is one of the best investments before buying a home or deciding on repair vs. replacement.

Frequently Asked Questions

How does a sewer camera inspection work?

We access your sewer line through a cleanout. The camera head is pushed through the line on a flexible cable. Video shows the pipe interior in real time, and a location transmitter marks problems from above ground.

Should I get one before buying a house?

In Chicago, absolutely. Standard home inspections don't include the sewer line. A $200–$400 camera inspection can save you from a $10,000+ surprise replacement after closing.

How long does an inspection take?

30–60 minutes. We show you the footage in real time and explain what we're seeing. You get a summary and our recommendation for next steps.

Can the camera reach the city main?

In most cases, yes. Our cameras travel 100+ feet. If a blockage prevents passage, we note the location and nature of the obstruction.

Do you provide a recording?

Yes. We provide video or photos for your records — especially useful for real estate transactions or insurance claims.

Need Sewer Camera Inspection?

Our licensed plumbers are available 24/7 for emergency and scheduled service.
